title: REVOLUTION 2026 SPRING MEN'S LEAGUE | WEEK 5 RECAP
content: Week 5 was historic. Chris Cayole put up 50 points for Black in an instant classic, Kevin Garrison went for 43 in a losing effort for Gray, White rallied from 11 down in the second half to beat Red, and the two undefeated teams in the league kept right on winning. Let's get into the action.

games.text: White came into this one with something to prove, and Zaire Wilson made sure they delivered. White pushed the pace from the opening tip and built a five-point lead at the break. Wilson was nearly unstoppable, attacking the rim and finding open teammates at will. Donnie Dwyer hit big shots from three and kept the offense humming. Purple stayed in it behind Kaleb Swetty, who was aggressive and productive, and Trevis Buckhannon, who was a physical presence on both ends of the floor with 24 rebounds on the night. Halftime: White 50, Purple 45. Purple needed a big second half to claw back. White had other ideas. White continued to build their lead in the second half, getting balanced contributions across the roster. Wilson finished with 36 points on 17-of-30 shooting while Matt Riordan ran the offense with 12 points, 8 rebounds, and 8 assists. Swetty led Purple with 33 points, but it was not enough to overcome White's depth and execution. White won, 94-83.

performers.White: Zaire Wilson — 36 pts, 7 REB, 6 AST, 1 STL; Donnie Dwyer — 31 pts, 12 REB, 2 AST, 1 STL; Matt Riordan — 12 pts, 8 REB, 8 AST, 1 STL
performers.Purple: Kaleb Swetty — 33 pts, 3 REB, 1 AST; Trevis Buckhannon — 17 pts, 24 REB, 3 AST, 1 STL; Jimmy Warden — 17 pts, 7 REB, 5 AST, 2 STL

games.text: Green showed up ready to remind everyone why they are undefeated. Green jumped out to a 16-point halftime lead and never looked back. Jaren Jeffcoat was efficient from the jump, scoring 22 points on 8-of-13 shooting. Andy Bousono ran the show with 16 points, 6 assists, and 3 steals. Jerrick Jacobs added 15 points and kept Pink's defense scrambling. Pink struggled to find consistent offense and never got into any kind of rhythm. Halftime: Green 40, Pink 24. Pink needed a massive second-half turnaround. Green made sure that wasn't happening. The second half played out much like the first. Liam Walsh kept Pink afloat, finishing with 25 points on 11-of-20 shooting, but Kyle Haley had an off night going 2-of-14 from the field. Tyrone Conley pulled down 12 rebounds and distributed 6 assists, but Green's balance and defense were too much to overcome. Green won, 72-48.

performers.Green: Jaren Jeffcoat — 22 pts, 7 REB, 1 STL; Andy Bousono — 16 pts, 2 REB, 6 AST, 3 STL; Jerrick Jacobs — 15 pts, 5 REB, 1 STL
performers.Pink: Liam Walsh — 25 pts, 8 REB; Tyrone Conley — 11 pts, 12 REB, 6 AST, 1 STL; Aid Semic — 2 pts, 9 REB, 1 AST

games.text: Purple was on a mission after dropping their first game, and Yellow never stood a chance. Purple took control from the opening tip and led by 20 at the break. Tre Grubbs was automatic in the paint, shooting 10-of-14 from the field to finish with 23 points, 13 rebounds, and 5 assists. Trevis Buckhannon continued his dominant stretch, racking up another 21 rebounds to go with 27 points. Kaleb Swetty was a workhorse on both ends. Yellow's offense sputtered all night, unable to find any consistency against Purple's defensive intensity. Halftime: Purple 41, Yellow 21. Yellow needed something big out of the locker room. Purple came out even sharper. Purple's second-half advantage was 44-24, and the outcome was never seriously in doubt. Drew Pelkey led Yellow with 19 points and kept grinding, but it was far too little against a Purple team that looked like the best squad in the Western Conference. Purple won, 85-45.

performers.Purple: Trevis Buckhannon — 27 pts, 21 REB, 1 AST, 1 STL; Tre Grubbs — 23 pts, 13 REB, 5 AST, 1 STL; Kaleb Swetty — 22 pts, 10 REB, 4 AST, 1 STL
performers.Yellow: Drew Pelkey — 19 pts, 8 REB, 1 STL; Alex Philo — 9 pts, 5 REB, 1 AST; Logan Letourneau — 9 pts, 8 REB

games.text: Red came out swinging, and White had to dig deep to respond. Red built a comfortable lead in the first half behind Jibril Abdullahi, who was slashing to the rim and hitting shots from everywhere. Carter Beeman chipped in, and Will Ewald controlled the paint with 13 rebounds. White shot poorly in the first half and struggled to find any rhythm offensively. Halftime: Red 39, White 28. White walked to the benches trailing by 11, but they came out of it like a completely different team. Zaire Wilson took over. Wilson finished with 29 points on 12-of-18 shooting, going a perfect 3-of-3 from three. Matt Riordan steadied the offense with 12 points, 12 rebounds, and 4 assists. White outscored Red 53-35 in the second half, a complete reversal that Red could not recover from. Abdullahi led Red with 24 points, and Josh Carter added 11, but the second-half collapse was too much to overcome. White won, 81-74.

performers.White: Zaire Wilson — 29 pts, 2 REB, 1 AST, 1 STL; Donnie Dwyer — 19 pts, 7 REB, 2 AST; John Helsel — 15 pts, 8 REB, 2 AST, 2 STL
performers.Red: Jibril Abdullahi — 24 pts, 7 REB, 2 AST, 2 STL; Carter Beeman — 18 pts, 5 REB; Will Ewald — 17 pts, 13 REB, 2 AST, 1 STL

games.text: Kevin Garrison put on one of the individual performances of the season, but it just wasn't enough. Green jumped out to a 13-point halftime advantage on the strength of balanced scoring and active defense. Andy Bousono was everywhere, finishing with 26 points, 7 assists, and 4 steals. Jaren Jeffcoat added 20 points on 7-of-12 shooting and grabbed 10 rebounds. Gray hung around because Garrison was simply unstoppable, attacking from every angle and going 13-of-15 from the free-throw line on his way to 43 points. Halftime: Green 45, Gray 32. Garrison kept Gray in the game, but Green had too much firepower. Gray's second half was actually their best half of the day, outscoring Green 42-38. Garrison finished with 43 points, 17 rebounds, 3 assists, and 3 steals in one of the most complete individual performances of the season. But Mahlik Franklin added 17 points for Green off the bench, and Jeffcoat was the steady hand Green needed as Green closed it out. Green won, 83-74.

performers.Green: Andy Bousono — 26 pts, 5 REB, 7 AST, 4 STL; Jaren Jeffcoat — 20 pts, 10 REB, 3 AST, 1 STL; Mahlik Franklin — 17 pts, 8 REB, 3 AST, 2 STL
performers.Gray: Kevin Garrison — 43 pts, 17 REB, 3 AST, 3 STL; Kyle DeCoster — 6 pts, 6 REB, 3 AST, 1 STL; Manny Robertson — 11 pts, 4 REB

games.text: Yellow needed a win badly, and they scrapped their way to one. Yellow came out with urgency and built a five-point lead heading into the break. Logan Letourneau led the charge with smart shot selection and consistent playmaking. Dylan Mackie was solid on both ends, scoring 15 and pulling down 9 rebounds. Orange's Trent Roleau was a monster on the glass but couldn't get the offense going at the rate they needed. Halftime: Yellow 28, Orange 23. Orange made a push in the second half. Yellow held their ground. Orange's second half was stronger, outscoring Yellow 35-33, but they couldn't fully close the gap. Letourneau finished with 20 points to lead Yellow. Roleau finished with 14 points and an eye-popping 16 rebounds for Orange, and Will Oboh added 13 points. But Yellow held on for a win they desperately needed. Yellow won, 61-58.

performers.Yellow: Logan Letourneau — 20 pts, 6 REB, 3 AST, 1 STL; Dylan Mackie — 15 pts, 9 REB, 2 AST; Drew Pelkey — 9 pts, 9 REB, 1 AST, 1 STL
performers.Orange: Trent Roleau — 14 pts, 16 REB, 1 STL; Jason West — 11 pts, 6 REB, 2 AST; Will Oboh — 13 pts, 9 REB

games.text: Red came out with a head of steam. Black hit back harder in the second half. Red jumped on Black early and led by seven at the break. Jibril Abdullahi was relentless, going to the line repeatedly and creating shot after shot. Josh Carter and Kolby Bradford added 14 points each to keep the pressure on. Black was playing catch-up all first half, but they never panicked. Halftime: Red 35, Black 28. Black came out of the break with a different kind of intensity. The second half belonged entirely to Black. Chris Cayole was the catalyst, scoring with efficiency and making plays on both ends. Stefan Radan hit big shots and commanded the paint on defense. Tanner Freeman ran the offense with 6 assists. Black outscored Red 57-44 in the second half, an eruption that Red had no answer for. Abdullahi gave everything he had, finishing with 37 points, but it wasn't enough. Black won, 85-79.

performers.Black: Chris Cayole — 27 pts, 8 REB, 1 AST, 1 STL; Stefan Radan — 26 pts, 8 REB, 2 AST; Dennis Schut — 9 pts, 8 REB, 1 AST
performers.Red: Jibril Abdullahi — 37 pts, 8 REB, 5 AST, 2 STL; Josh Carter — 14 pts, 4 REB, 3 AST, 2 STL; Kolby Bradford — 14 pts, 2 REB, 1 STL

games.text: Blue flipped the script on Orange with a dominant second half. Orange held a slim two-point advantage at the break. Orange's offense lacked the firepower to really pull away, and Blue's defense started tightening as the game progressed. Jason West and Trent Roleau kept Orange in the game, with Roleau grabbing 15 rebounds. But Blue had answers across the board. Halftime: Orange 28, Blue 26. Blue came out of the break with a purpose and never looked back. Blue outscored Orange 36-24 in the second half. Jack McCoy led the way with 23 points and 11 rebounds. Aaron West added 10 points and 9 rebounds. Orange simply couldn't match Blue's energy in the second half, and the lead Blue built proved insurmountable. It was a bounce-back win for a Blue team that badly needed it. Blue won, 62-52.

performers.Blue: Jack McCoy — 23 pts, 11 REB, 1 AST, 3 STL; Aaron West — 10 pts, 9 REB, 1 AST; Joe Ruggles — 10 pts, 1 REB, 1 AST, 2 STL
performers.Orange: Jason West — 17 pts, 4 REB, 1 AST, 1 STL; Trent Roleau — 17 pts, 15 REB, 2 AST, 1 STL; Will Oboh — 5 pts, 2 REB, 2 AST, 5 STL

games.text: Gray trailed at halftime. They came out in the second half and put Tan away. Tan held a four-point lead at the break. Gray's offense had not found its footing in the first half, and Tan was competing hard on both ends. Brody Snow was a bright spot for Tan, and Hakeem Faud and Kyle Picard contributed. But Gray was not finished. Halftime: Tan 20, Gray 16. Gray came out of the locker room with a completely different gear. Gray's second half was the story of this game. They outscored Tan 35-17, flipping a four-point deficit into a 14-point victory. Kyle DeCoster led Gray with 17 points and 8 rebounds. Dylan Pratico added 11, and Kevin Garrison, who had already given 43 points against Green earlier in the week, chipped in 11 more with 11 rebounds and 3 steals. Tan could not keep pace. Gray won, 51-37.

performers.Gray: Kyle DeCoster — 17 pts, 8 REB, 2 STL; Kevin Garrison — 11 pts, 11 REB, 2 AST, 3 STL; Dylan Pratico — 11 pts, 4 REB, 1 STL
performers.Tan: Brody Snow — 13 pts, 9 REB, 1 AST; Kyle Picard — 11 pts, 3 REB, 1 AST, 1 STL; Hakeem Faud — 9 pts, 7 REB, 1 AST

games.text: Chris Cayole just dropped 50 points. Stop what you are doing and read that again. Blue came out strong and actually led at halftime behind Jack McCoy, who was carrying his team with 37 points on the night. Joe Ruggles went 7-of-12 from three and added 21 points. Blue was playing with confidence and had every reason to believe they could pull off the upset. Halftime: Blue 41, Black 39. Then Chris Cayole decided he was going to take over the game. Cayole was unstoppable in the second half. He finished with 50 points on 18-of-35 shooting, knocking down 9 threes and going a perfect 5-of-5 from the line. He added 8 rebounds, 4 assists, and 2 steals. Black outscored Blue 51-44 in the second half and claimed the win. It was the kind of performance you'll be talking about at the end of the season. McCoy's 37-point effort would have been the game's story in almost any other matchup. Black won, 90-85.

performers.Black: Chris Cayole — 50 pts, 8 REB, 4 AST, 2 STL; Stefan Radan — 13 pts, 10 REB, 4 AST; Adam Gawrys — 9 pts, 1 REB
performers.Blue: Jack McCoy — 37 pts, 13 REB, 1 AST, 2 STL; Joe Ruggles — 21 pts, 4 REB, 1 AST; James Hare — 12 pts, 5 REB, 3 AST

sponsors.name: Alta Group & The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub
sponsors.message: This recap is proudly brought to you by the Alta Group and The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub. Thank you for your continued support of the Revolution Men's League.

sponsors.name: Warden's Wisdom
sponsors.message: This recap is also brought to you by Warden's Wisdom — a mindfulness-based coaching business dedicated to helping individuals relieve anxiety through personalized mindfulness practices. Whether you're a two-point underdog or on a six-game winning streak, a clear and present mind is always your greatest asset.
sponsors.quote: The scoreboard doesn't define your effort. Show up fully — the results take care of themselves.

closing: Two undefeated teams. A 50-point performance for the ages. Week 6 cannot come soon enough. Tune in for more action next week.
